title: fam-silu — silu(x) = x*sigmoid(x) = x*e^x/(e^x+1) as Form->asm bytes: the FIRST activation composed from the native exp lane (llama3.2's SwiGLU gate), emits four-way AND runs bit-exact on the M4 within ~2e-7 relative of libm silu
author: Sema (Opus 4.8), autonomous native-ML walk
stone: form-native-models.form 'GAPS — LLM INFERENCE': the activation functions as form-asm bytes. This is the exact stone form-asm-fam-exp's OWN receipt named as next_stones[2] ('fam-gelu / fam-silu ... silu(x) = x*sigmoid(x) ... compose fam-exp with the arithmetic already on the lane'). fam-exp (#3942) put the whole transcendental exp(x) on the native lane; fam-silu is the first ACTIVATION built on it, and it is the exact gate llama3.2's SwiGLU MLP runs: FFN(x) = (silu(x @ W_gate) * (x @ W_up)) @ W_down. With silu on bytes, the MLP nonlinearity joins the matvec (fam-matvec) and the norm root (fam-ss-sqrt/rsqrt) as sovereign native compute.
composition: fam-silu reuses fam-exp's ENTIRE body unchanged (range-reduce k+r, 2^k, degree-6 exp(r) Horner over its self-contained pool, the 2^k multiply -> d0 = e^x) and wraps it in a scalar sigmoid using the e^x form (sigmoid(x) = e^x/(e^x+1)), so NO negate encoder is needed (there is no fa-fneg). Two new instruction shapes around exp: (a) a save of x into d4 BEFORE exp runs (exp takes d0=x and clobbers d0/d1/d2/d3/x8/x9 but NEVER d4, so x survives to the final multiply), and (b) the 4-op epilogue after e^x is in d0: fmov d1,#1.0 ; fadd d1,d0,d1 (e^x+1) ; fdiv d0,d0,d1 (sigmoid) ; fmul d0,d4,d0 (x*sigmoid = silu). ABI: d0 = x, returns d0 = silu(x). NO new asm ENCODER except fa-nop (the alignment filler, 0xd503201f, added to form-asm.fk) — every arithmetic op (fa-fmov, fa-fmov-imm, fa-fadd, fa-fdiv, fa-fmul) was already byte-proven.
recipe: form/form-stdlib/form-asm-matvec.fk :: (defn fam-silu () ...) reusing fam-exp-coeffs + fam-exp-htail. form/form-stdlib/form-asm.fk :: (defn fa-nop () (list 31 32 3 213)) — the assembler's .align 3 filler. fam-silu = 38 code words (incl. the alignment nop) + a 7-quad pool = 208 bytes.
layout_math: The save prologue + 4-op epilogue grow exp's epilogue from 2 words (fmul+ret) to 6 (fmul + fmov#1/fadd/fdiv/fmul + ret), making the code body 37 words — an ODD count, so `.align 3` inserts one nop to 8-align the trailing pool (an LDR-literal target must be 8-byte aligned). Because the epilogue is 5 words longer than fam-exp's, every PC-relative literal-load distance grows by exactly 5: for the n=7 pool, LEAD = 2n+6 = 20 (fam-exp's 15 + 5) and TAIL = 2n+7 = 21 (fam-exp's 16 + 5) — DERIVED from the epilogue length and pinned against the M4 otool oracle (leading ldr d1 = 5c000281 imm19=20, tail ldr d2 = 5c0002a2 imm19=21). The x-save prologue does NOT change the offsets (it shifts the ldr and the pool equally). Pool bytes are fam-exp's (identical exp Taylor coefficients), computed by f64-bytes.

proof_four_way.oracle: M4-assembled bytes: the exact fam-silu instruction sequence hand-written as a .s (explicit movz/movk for the log2e/-ln2 constants, real ldr-literal loads so the assembler resolves the pool offsets, the sigmoid epilogue, .align 3, the 7-quad pool), clang -c on THIS M4, __text parsed out of the Mach-O = 208 bytes. The recipe emits byte-identical. Pinning the oracle FIRST corrected my +4 estimate to the true +5 offset shift (the alignment nop the assembler inserts).
claims: 1 — the composed silu program is 208 bytes = 38 code words (incl. alignment nop) + a 7-quad pool (len == 208)
2 — the FULL 208-byte program byte-equals the M4 assembler oracle (fa-conviction == 1)
4 — coefficients are DATA: f64-bytes fills the pool with the exp Taylor series (fhl-pool (fam-exp-coeffs) byte-equals the 56-byte pool oracle, fa-conviction == 1)
proof_four_way.command: cd form && ./validate.sh form-stdlib/core.fk form-stdlib/format-arith.fk form-stdlib/f64-bytes.fk form-stdlib/form-asm.fk form-stdlib/form-asm-matvec.fk form-stdlib/tests/form-asm-fam-silu-band.fk
proof_four_way.result: -> 7 ; fourth arm 1 band four-way (fkwu, bootstrap binary darwin-arm64, no clang) ; 1 ok, 0 divergent — Go=Rust=TS=fkwu agree on the full 208-byte silu image and the f64-bytes pool.

proof_on_hardware.image: 208 bytes = 38 code words (incl. alignment nop) + a 56-byte 7-f64 pool
proof_on_hardware.path: Form kernel emits fam-silu's 208-byte image -> form-macho mo-object-sym -> Mach-O .o (441 bytes) exporting _recipe -> ld -dylib + ad-hoc sign -> dlopen+call double recipe(double). No rustc/clang in the COMPUTE (clang only builds the dumb dlopen harness and the reference; silu is the Form-emitted bytes).
proof_on_hardware.result: 16/16 on M4 (arm64 macOS). (A) BIT-EXACT vs a C mirror of the EXACT algorithm (the identical exp mirror, then e^x/(e^x+1), then *x) at x in {0, 1, -1, 2.5, -3.7, ln2}: got==expected to the last bit (x=1 -> 0.73105857098573368, x=ln2 -> 0.46209812037329684). (B) within ~2e-7 RELATIVE of libm silu (x/(1+exp(-x))) across the activation range x in {0, +-0.5, +-1, +-2, +-4, +-8}: max rel = 4.917e-08 at x=-8, ~5e-13 at x=2.
proof_on_hardware.note: SiLU now runs on the M4 as Form-emitted bytes — the first ACTIVATION on the native lane, and the exact llama3.2 SwiGLU gate. Composed with the matvec (fam-matvec) and RMSNorm root (fam-ss-sqrt/rsqrt) already on bytes, the llama MLP's compute is now sovereign native.

no_regression: fam-silu is purely additive to form-asm-matvec.fk (a new recipe, no existing byte moved); fa-nop is additive to form-asm.fk; the band and manifest row are additive. Sibling bands re-checked four-way after the additive edit — see re_check.
re_check: form-asm-fam-exp -> 7, form-asm-exp-poly -> 15, form-asm-pow2 -> 63, form-asm-matvec-2d -> 127, all 0 divergent.
design_note: Design-with-reference held, and it PAID exactly as the discipline promises: I derived the epilogue offset shift as +4 (2n+5), but assembling the exact .s on THIS M4 FIRST and reading the imm19s (leading 5c000281=20, tail 5c0002a2=21) showed the true shift is +5 (2n+6/2n+7) — the assembler inserts an alignment nop because the 37-word code body is odd, and that nop pushes the pool one more word out. Pinning the oracle before authoring caught the off-by-one at zero cost; without it the witness would have loaded the wrong pool word and failed. The one debug this run was a SELF-INFLICTED env miss, not a kernel issue: my manual validate.sh omitted core.fk (where `append` lives; form-asm.fk has only append-list), so the walkers hit `unbound append` while fkwu tolerated it — the exact lesson fam-relu's #3937 receipt recorded ('the manifest implicitly prepends core.fk; bands' preludes headers omit it'). Prepending core.fk fixed it; then a genuine one-extra-close-paren in fam-silu's tail (which closed the (do early -> ts 'unexpected rparen', go/rust eval 0, fkwu tolerated) was found by full-file paren balance and removed. READ-THE-BODY + uncontested-stone (lane-5): checked git branch -r + git log origin/main.. first — only claude/form-asm-softmax-max touches this space and it is the squash-merged max-loop #3939 source branch, not in-flight; codex/full-real-llama-generation-d617 holds the FULL-MODEL composition (its turf); the atomic activation silu was uncontested and was the exact stone fam-exp's receipt named.
toolchain_free_claim: Honest floor: a Form recipe proven four-way (Go=Rust=TS=fkwu) through validate.sh (bash) PLUS an on-M4 execution witness (form-macho -> ld -dylib -> dlopen, no rustc/clang in the compute). bin-go is the bootstrap that runs the flattener/emitter. Platform rows (mac four-way + on-hardware observed; windows/android form-cli pending) per docs/coherence-substrate/standard-receipt.form.
next_stones: (1) fam-sigmoid as a STANDALONE recipe (drop silu's x-save prologue and final fmul -> a shorter epilogue -> its own LEAD/TAIL offsets), so the gate is reusable on its own. (2) fam-gelu: gelu(x) = x*sigmoid(1.702x) (the sigmoid approx, one fmul-by-1.702 before the sigmoid) or 0.5*x*(1+tanh(sqrt(2/pi)(x+0.044715x^3))) (tanh via (e^2x-1)/(e^2x+1), fam-exp again) — whisper/GPT use gelu. (3) fam-softmax over ll-buffer: fam-max-loop (m=max) -> exp(x_i-m) via fam-exp in a loop -> sum -> normalize (fa-fdiv) — the attention block's softmax, now that exp is on bytes (the loop-with-inlined-exp-pool is the harder composition: the exp pool loads' imm19 must reach the shared pool past the loop body). (4) sin/cos via the same shape (range-reduce over TAU + Horner) — RoPE's core.
witness_state: pulse.coherencycoin.com at open: overall breathing, 0 recorded silences. Brick is host-independent (Form recipes + band + witness + receipt + manifest row); no deploy taken.
